items.title: CMS issues guidance on new limits for federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds for certain noncitizens starting Oct. 1, 2026
items.summary: CMS says states must implement a statutory change starting October 1, 2026 that limits when states can claim federal matching funds in Medicaid and CHIP for certain noncitizens. CMS notes the change does not affect federal matching funds for emergency medical conditions and does not change states’ option to cover lawfully residing children and pregnant people in Medicaid/CHIP.

items.title: KFF: states prepare for major Medicaid/CHIP policy changes (work requirements, 6-month renewals, immigrant eligibility changes)
items.summary: A KFF/Georgetown survey (policies as of January 2026) summarizes major changes in the 2025 reconciliation law, including immigrant eligibility limits beginning October 2026 and work/community engagement requirements plus 6-month renewals for ACA expansion adults beginning January 2027. KFF also highlights a future cost-sharing change starting October 2028 that would require cost-sharing for non-exempt services up to $35 per service for some expansion adults.

items.title: HUD notice: PHAs can apply for $10 million in additional HUD-VASH administrative fees; applications due April 14, 2026
items.summary: HUD’s PIH 2026-01 (Revised) notice makes $10 million available in additional administrative fees for public housing agencies (PHAs) administering HUD-VASH vouchers. HUD says applications must be received by midnight April 14, 2026 (in the PHA’s time zone) and the funds must be used only for HUD-VASH-related administrative and leasing support activities.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

items.title: USDA WIC policy memo updates the maximum monthly allowance for milk for FY 2026; authority ends Sept. 30, 2026
items.summary: USDA issued WIC Policy Memorandum 2026-1 directing state agencies to implement updated WIC maximum monthly allowances (MMAs) of fluid milk for FY 2026 (including 16 quarts for Food Packages IV and VI, 22 quarts for Food Package V, and 24 quarts for Food Package VII). USDA says the authority for the updated amounts terminates September 30, 2026.

items.title: Federal Register: ACF seeks OMB extension and changes to LIHEAP Quarterly Reports; comments were due April 3, 2026
items.summary: A Federal Register notice says the Administration for Children and Families is requesting a 3-year extension of the LIHEAP Quarterly Reports information collection (OMB control number 0970-0589) with proposed changes intended to reduce redundancy and administrative burden. The notice set a public comment deadline of April 3, 2026.

items.title: IRS publishes 2026 inflation adjustments: maximum EITC rises to $8,231 for families with 3+ qualifying children
items.summary: IRS Revenue Procedure 2025-32 lists the maximum earned income tax credit (EITC) for 2026 as $664 (no qualifying children), $4,427 (one child), $7,316 (two children), and $8,231 (three or more children). The same document says taxpayers generally cannot claim the EITC if their investment income exceeds $12,200.

items.title: Federal Student Aid: 2025–26 FAFSA must be received by June 30, 2026
items.summary: Federal Student Aid’s 2025–26 FAFSA materials state that applications must be received no later than June 30, 2026 for federal student aid. The same document warns state and school deadlines can be earlier, so students should submit as early as possible.
items.source: Federal Student Aid (U.S. Department of Education)

items.title: Overview: major SNAP changes rolling out in 2026 (work rules, waiver restrictions, nutrition-focused purchase limits)
items.summary: An AEI analysis summarizes changes expected in 2026 following recent federal actions, including tighter SNAP work requirements and waiver limits, plus state-level nutrition-focused purchase restrictions under USDA-approved waivers. The piece notes some approved purchase restrictions were set to begin as early as January 1, 2026, with additional restrictions rolling out over the course of 2026.

items.title: Social Security COLA page: 2026 cost-of-living adjustment is 2.8%
items.summary: SSA’s COLA information page lists the 2026 cost-of-living adjustment (COLA) as 2.8%. COLA-related payment changes generally start with December payments for SSI recipients (paid at the end of November) and January payments for Social Security beneficiaries.
